chapter_title: Clocks and watches and parts thereof
inclusions: Heading 91.01 — Wrist-watches, pocket-watches and other watches, including stop-watches, with case WHOLLY of precious metal, metal clad with precious metal, or same materials combined with natural/cultured pearls or precious/semi-precious stones (headings 71.01–71.04). Note 2: watches with base metal case INLAID with precious metal → 91.02
Heading 91.02 — Wrist-watches, pocket-watches and other watches, including stop-watches, NOT of heading 91.01 (i.e., cases of base metal, base metal inlaid with precious metal, plastics, etc.)
Heading 91.03 — Clocks with watch movements (movements not exceeding 12 mm thickness and 50 mm width/length/diameter — Note 3)
Heading 91.04 — Instrument panel clocks and clocks of a similar type for vehicles, aircraft, spacecraft or vessels
Heading 91.05 — Other clocks (alarm clocks, wall clocks, cuckoo clocks, etc.)

Heading 91.06 — Time of day recording apparatus and apparatus for measuring, recording or otherwise indicating intervals of time, with clock or watch movement (time stamps, time recorders, parking meters with timers)
Heading 91.07 — Time switches with clock or watch movement or with synchronous motor
Heading 91.08 — Watch movements, complete and assembled (movements per Note 3: regulated by balance-wheel/hairspring, quartz crystal or other system; ≤ 12 mm thick and ≤ 50 mm wide/long/diameter)
Heading 91.09 — Clock movements, complete and assembled
Heading 91.10 — Complete watch or clock movements, unassembled or partly assembled (movement sets); incomplete watch or clock movements, assembled; rough watch or clock movements
Heading 91.11 — Watch cases and parts thereof
Heading 91.12 — Clock cases and cases of a similar type for other goods of Chapter 91, and parts thereof
Heading 91.13 — Watch straps, watch bands and watch bracelets, and parts thereof (of any material including leather, textile, base metal, precious metal)
Heading 91.14 — Other clock or watch parts (including clock or watch springs — Note 1(c) override)

exclusions.action: Classify according to constituent material (e.g., mineral glass → Ch.70; sapphire/synthetic crystal → Ch.71; plastics → Ch.39) — Note 1(a)
Classify according to constituent material (e.g., cast iron weight → Ch.73; lead weight → Ch.78) — Note 1(a)
Redirect to heading 71.13 (jewellery, if of precious metal) or 71.17 (imitation jewellery) — Note 1(b). Watch STRAPS/BANDS → stay in Chapter 91 (91.13)

exclusions.condition: Parts of general use (as defined in Note 2 to Section XV) of base metal, or similar goods of plastics or of precious metal/metal clad with precious metal — EXCEPT clock/watch springs
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XV (base metal), Chapter 39 (plastics), or heading 71.15 (precious metal) — Note 1(c). CRITICAL EXCEPTION: clock or watch SPRINGS → always heading 91.14 regardless of this rule
Redirect to heading 73.26 (steel balls) or 84.82 (ball bearings as assemblies) — Note 1(d)

exclusions.condition: Articles of heading 84.12 constructed to work WITHOUT AN ESCAPEMENT (e.g., sand timers, non-escapement time-measuring devices)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 84.12 — Note 1(e). The escapement mechanism is a defining feature of 'clock or watch' movements; without it → 84.12

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 85 NOT YET assembled together or with other components into watch/clock movements or into articles suitable for use solely/principally as parts of such movements
exclusions.action: Remain in Chapter 85 — Note 1(g). Electronic/electrical components (oscillators, integrated circuits, motors) stay in Ch.85 until assembled into a clock/watch movement or dedicated clock/watch part

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — 91.01 vs 91.02: case material determines heading for watches
classification_rules.description: The watch case material is the sole dividing criterion between 91.01 and 91.02: heading 91.01 = case WHOLLY of precious metal, metal clad with precious metal, or these materials combined with natural/cultured pearls or precious/semi-precious stones (71.01–71.04). ALL OTHER watch cases (base metal, base metal INLAID with precious metal, stainless steel, titanium, plastics, rubber) → 91.02. Inlaid ≠ wholly of precious metal; even a partially inlaid case → 91.02.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Definition of 'watch movement' for heading 91.08 classification
classification_rules.description: A 'watch movement' (91.08) must be: (a) regulated by a balance-wheel and hairspring, quartz crystal or any other system capable of determining intervals of time; (b) with a display or a system to which a mechanical display can be incorporated; AND (c) NOT exceeding 12 mm in THICKNESS and 50 mm in WIDTH, LENGTH or DIAMETER. If either dimension limit is exceeded → the movement is a 'clock movement' (91.09), not a watch movement.

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — Dual-use movements/parts: clock/watch heading takes priority
classification_rules.description: When movements or parts are suitable for use BOTH in clocks/watches AND in other articles (e.g., precision instruments), they are classified in Chapter 91 — EXCEPT where Note 1 explicitly excludes them. This overrides the general parts-follow-principal-use rule. Example: a precision quartz oscillator usable in both a watch and a precision instrument → classified in Ch.91 once assembled into/for a clock/watch movement.

Note 1(c) excludes 'parts of general use' from Chapter 91 — but explicitly carves out clock and watch springs, which MUST be classified in heading 91.14 regardless of their general-purpose nature. Springs for clocks/watches are always Ch.91 parts, even though structurally similar springs may go to Section XV.

Watch STRAPS, BANDS and BRACELETS (including parts) of any material → heading 91.13, regardless of material (leather, textile, rubber, base metal, precious metal). Watch CHAINS (linked chain style, typically precious metal) → 71.13 (jewellery) or 71.17 (imitation jewellery) per Note 1(b). The form factor distinguishes: articulated strap/bracelet (worn flat, clasped) → 91.13; standalone chain with fob/pendant style → 71.13/71.17.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 91.03 (clock with watch movement) vs 91.05 (other clocks)
classification_rules.description: Heading 91.03 covers clocks whose timekeeping mechanism IS a watch movement as defined in Note 3 (≤12 mm thick, ≤50 mm diameter). A miniature mantel clock or desk clock with a tiny quartz watch-sized movement → 91.03. Standard alarm clocks, wall clocks with larger clock movements → 91.05. Classification hinges on the physical dimensions of the internal movement, not the size of the clock case.

classification_rules.rule: Ch.85 electrical components → Chapter 91 threshold: 'assembled into movement'
classification_rules.description: Per Note 1(g), Ch.85 components (stepper motors, quartz oscillators, integrated circuits for timekeeping) stay in Chapter 85 UNTIL they are assembled into a watch/clock movement or into articles suitable for principally serving as parts of such movements. The assembly threshold determines the chapter: unassembled components → Ch.85; assembled into a movement or movement-dedicated subassembly → Ch.91.
